care.summary: A popular dracaena with a thick woody cane and clusters of arching, strap-like leaves with a central yellow stripe.
care.light: Low to medium indirect light; tolerates fluorescent office lighting well
care.water: Water when top half of soil is dry; sensitive to overwatering and fluoride
care.soil: Well-draining potting mix with perlite or sand
care.humidity: Low to average; adapts well to normal indoor humidity
care.fertilizer: Dilute balanced fertilizer every 6 weeks spring through summer
care.toxicity: Toxic to cats and dogs if ingested
common_issues: Brown leaf tips from fluoride in tap water
Root rot from overwatering or poor drainage
Yellow lower leaves — natural aging or overwatering sign
tips: Use filtered or distilled water to prevent leaf tip burn
Prune the cane at desired height — new growth will emerge below cut
Remove lower yellow leaves to keep stem clean and tidy
Rotate monthly to maintain even upright growth
